comparisons.visual: Gaslight on high shelves. JONATHAN HARKER, late twenties, neat brown hair, clean-shaven English solicitor in a dark traveling suit, bends over maps and heavy fo…
Jonathan scribbles in his journal.
At the door, LANDLADY ELISABETA-elderly, cheery-looking, white undergarment with long double apron of coloured stuff fitting almost too tight for modesty-bows a…
Josef and Elisabeta cross themselves. Fear tightens both faces.
She goes out. Jonathan stands alone, the crucifix on his breast. Coach horn distant below.
The calèche tears through blue-black forest. Wolves howl on every side-blue flames flicker among the trees. Jonathan grips the seat. The Driver raises a hand; t…
The handshake so akin to the Driver's that Jonathan stares.
A small octagonal room lit by a single lamp. Seemingly without a window. The Count opens a further door and motions Jonathan through.
The Count takes the cover from a dish. Excellent roast chicken, cheese, salad, a bottle of old Tokay. Jonathan eats. Two glasses of wine. During the meal the Co…
Early morning light-7 May. Jonathan enters dressed. A cold breakfast laid out; coffee kept hot on the hearth. A card on the table.
Jonathan draws papers. The Count signs with a hand that never warms.
Below-the great door shuts. The Count has returned.
He holds up his arms.
Another night. Books open. The Count questions Jonathan on legal matters-methodical, precise.
Jonathan, restless, finds a door unlocked that was not before. He enters a dusty chamber long unused-old furniture, moth-eaten hangings, a great bed. Moonlight …
He rises, pale, and forces himself toward the door.
Jonathan writes. His face goes bloodless.
Jonathan reads by the window. The door opens. Count Dracula enters, smoothest voice, two letters in his hand. He sits beside Jonathan.
Jonathan lies down. Exhaustion takes him. No dreams.
Before morning. Jonathan has run up the winding stair. He watches from the south window. Below and across: from the Count's window, a figure emerges-the whole m…
He knots sheets, climbs to the window ledge, and lowers himself into the abyss of night.
A distant clock strikes ten. She starts, sanding the letter.
Seward almost sits upon his silk hat, catches himself, and thereafter turns a lancet absently between his fingers until Lucy's eyes flick to it with a suppresse…
She laughs through sudden tears, seals the letter in haste.
He bows and goes. Lucy sinks onto the sofa, crying quietly, letter-paper already blotted beside her.
Lucy's eyes fill. She nods.
DR. JOHN SEWARD at his phonograph, speaking into the tube-professional mask cracked by private pain.
A bright sitting-room with sea-light at the windows. MRS. WESTENRA greets Mina warmly, then gathers gloves and card-case.
Days later. Bright sun. LUCY WESTENRA in white lawn frock, beautiful colour in her face, sits with MINA MURRAY. MR. SWALES and his two cronies lose no time join…
Lucy leans over; the letters face her.
Sultry August light. Holiday-makers thin along the cliff walk. High in the north-west sky, mares'-tails streak the blue. COASTGUARD DONOVAN stands with his spy-…
No one on the little pier at first. COASTGUARD DONOVAN runs down from the eastern side, first to climb aboard. Searchlight men scour the entrance, then hold the…
Mate's watch. One hand, PETROFSKY, relieved-does not go to bunk. Dawn: he is missing. Crew downcast.
Fog thick. Captain wakes to a cry outside his port. Rushes on deck-runs against Mate Dragomir. Man of watch gone. One more.
After the storm. Mina and Lucy on their seat. Below, the wrecked schooner Demeter lies under the East Cliff. Crowds stare. The immense dog is not found. MR. SWA…
Mina throws on clothes, snatches a big heavy shawl, runs out.
They hide in a doorway as a not-quite-sober man passes. Heartbeats loud. Home without meeting another soul.
Mina wakes. Lucy sitting up in bed, still asleep, pointing to the window. Mina pulls aside the blind. Brilliant moonlight. Between her and the moon a great bat …
Lucy moving back to bed, fast asleep, breathing heavily, hand to her throat. Mina does not wake her. Tucks her up. Door locked, window fastened. Lucy pale, draw…
Acknowledgment. Goods delivered. Keys left in main hall. Overplus returned.
